metadata.purpose: Machine-readable detection rules for the 3 new taxonomy categories identified in the 30-CRL verification audit. Rules are designed for automated CRL text classification pipelines.

detection_rules.taxonomy_name: PMA device component deficiencies blocking NDA/BLA approval

prerequisite_filter.description: Only evaluate this rule for combination product applications
signals: Application involves a drug-device combination product
Product has both drug and device components requiring separate regulatory review
CDRH involvement indicated anywhere in the submission record

high_confidence_signals.signal: Explicit PMA reference with Not Approvable outcome
high_confidence_signals.example_text: the device component of this combination product received a Not Approvable letter on the PMA on March 11, 2016
the original HF deficiencies included in the Not Approvable letter issued by FDA's Center for Devices and Radiological Health (CDRH)

high_confidence_signals.signal: NDA cannot be approved pending device PMA
high_confidence_signals.example_text: until such time as the device is determined to be approvable for use in combination with your proposed drug under this NDA, your NDA for the drug-device combination product cannot be approved
HF deficiencies identified by CDRH on February 9, 2017, with respect to the PMA resubmission to CDRH, remain outstanding

supporting_signals.note: Also present in CAT-07; treat as supporting signal only

exclusion_rules.exclusion: If PMA is mentioned only in HF context without blocking language, classify as CAT-07 not CAT-11-A
exclusion_rules.note: Pure HF deficiencies without PMA not-approvable determination → CAT-07

detection_rules.co_occurrence_note: CAT-11-A frequently co-occurs with CAT-07 (Human Factors). Both should be tagged when a CRL cites a CDRH PMA Not Approvable letter that includes HF deficiencies. CAT-11-A is the primary tag (regulatory blocking); CAT-07 is secondary.
detection_rules.ectd_lookup: Cross-reference application type in Module 1 cover letter for 'combination product' designation. Check CDRH database for PMA application associated with the same product.
detection_rules.false_positive_risk: LOW — 'PMA' + 'not approvable' combination is highly specific to this deficiency type

high_confidence_signals.note: This is a formal titled section in the CRL — presence is near-100% reliable

high_confidence_signals.example_text: Section 505(o)(3) of the FDCA authorizes FDA to require holders of approved drug and biological product applications to conduct postmarketing studies and clinical trials
an analysis of spontaneous postmarketing adverse events reported under subsection 505(k)(1) of the FDCA will not be sufficient to assess these serious risks
the new pharmacovigilance system that FDA is required to establish under section 505(k)(3) of the FDCA will not be sufficient to assess these serious risks

supporting_signals.signal: Formal 'you will be required to conduct' language
supporting_signals.pattern: (?i)you will be required to conduct the following (postmarketing|study|studies|clinical trial)
supporting_signals.note: Distinguishes mandatory PMR from voluntary PMC commitments

exclusion_rules.exclusion: General REMS requirements without 505(o)(3) citation → CAT-04-G, not CAT-09-C
exclusion_rules.note: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategies are separate from PMR — assign CAT-04-G

exclusion_rules.exclusion: Voluntary postmarketing commitment language → do not assign CAT-09-C
exclusion_rules.pattern: (?i)(encouraged to|may wish to|we recommend that you).{0,200}(postmarket|study|trial)
exclusion_rules.note: Voluntary PMC language does not trigger CAT-09-C

detection_rules.co_occurrence_note: CAT-09-C (PMR) can co-occur with CAT-04-B (efficacy failure), CAT-05-B (carcinogenicity), and CAT-04-E (risk-benefit). In such cases, all applicable categories should be tagged. CAT-09-C specifically captures the formal PMR instrument, not the underlying scientific deficiency.
detection_rules.boundary_with_cat04g: CAT-04-G = REMS requirements and general postmarket study requests (506B/FDAAA) without 505(o)(3) formal process. CAT-09-C = formal statutory PMR under 505(o)(3) with explicit 505(k)(1) and 505(k)(3) insufficiency findings. The 505(o)(3) section header is the definitive discriminator.
detection_rules.false_positive_risk: VERY LOW — section header 'POSTMARKETING REQUIREMENTS UNDER 505(O)(3)' is unique and unambiguous

high_confidence_signals.note: Distinct from 'FACILITY INSPECTIONS' section (CAT-02). Presence of this specific header is near-100% reliable.

high_confidence_signals.example_text: for-cause good clinical practice (GCP) inspections for Protocol MW2010-03-02, significant issues related to the conduct of the trial and GCP noncompliance were observed
significant unblinding events that occurred during the conduct of the study

supporting_signals.signal: Impact on data interpretability from site-level issues
supporting_signals.example_text: data generated from the inspected sites of poor quality

supporting_signals.signal: Study conduct issues at specific protocol sites

supporting_signals.note: BIMO = Bioresearch Monitoring; FDA program that inspects clinical investigators

supporting_signals.example_text: study data generated should be evaluated as if they were obtained from an open label study

exclusion_rules.exclusion: Manufacturing facility GMP inspection → CAT-02, not CAT-04-H
exclusion_rules.note: Key discriminator: 'CLINICAL SITE INSPECTIONS' vs 'FACILITY INSPECTIONS' — fundamentally different deficiency types

exclusion_rules.exclusion: COVID-19 travel restriction preventing facility inspection → CAT-02-C, not CAT-04-H
exclusion_rules.note: COVID-related inspection delays are a facility inspection issue (CAT-02-C), not a GCP conduct issue

exclusion_rules.exclusion: Clinical pharmacology site issues without GCP language → do not assign CAT-04-H
exclusion_rules.note: Pharmacokinetic study site issues without GCP noncompliance findings → evaluate under CAT-06

detection_rules.co_occurrence_note: CAT-04-H frequently co-occurs with CAT-04-B (efficacy failure) when GCP inspection findings impair the reliability of efficacy data, and with CAT-04-C (adequate/well-controlled studies) when study conduct failures undermine study design validity. Tag all applicable categories.
detection_rules.boundary_with_cat02: Critical distinction: CAT-02 covers GMP inspections of MANUFACTURING facilities (drug substance/product plants). CAT-04-H covers GCP inspections of CLINICAL INVESTIGATOR SITES (hospitals, clinics where trials were conducted). The CRL section header is the definitive discriminator: 'FACILITY INSPECTIONS' = CAT-02; 'CLINICAL SITE INSPECTIONS' = CAT-04-H.
detection_rules.temporal_note: For-cause GCP inspections are typically triggered by concerns identified during application review. Frequency may increase in years when FDA's BIMO program is more active or when major efficacy concerns prompt deeper site-level scrutiny.
detection_rules.false_positive_risk: LOW — 'CLINICAL SITE INSPECTIONS' header is distinct from facility inspection language

trigger_pattern.description: CRL NONCLINICAL section discusses impurity specifications exceeding ICH Q3B(R2) thresholds
trigger_pattern.additional_context: The CRL section is headed 'NONCLINICAL' but the content discusses impurity levels that require toxicological qualification per ICH Q3B(R2) — the root deficiency is CMC/impurity specification, not a standalone nonclinical study gap

disambiguation_logic.condition: NONCLINICAL section discusses ICH Q3B(R2) impurity qualification thresholds
NONCLINICAL section discusses missing genotoxicity/carcinogenicity/reproductive tox studies (ICH S-series guidance) without impurity context
disambiguation_logic.action: primary=CAT-05-A (or CAT-05-B/C/D as appropriate), no secondary

disambiguation_logic.condition: NONCLINICAL section discusses both impurity qualification AND standalone safety study gaps
disambiguation_logic.action: primary=CAT-03-G + relevant CAT-05 subcategory, all as co-primary

implementation_notes.pipeline_order: Apply detection rules in this order: (1) Section header detection for CLINICAL SITE INSPECTIONS, POSTMARKETING REQUIREMENTS UNDER 505(O)(3), and device/PMA language — these are near-definitive. (2) Apply supporting signal scoring if section headers are absent. (3) Apply miscategorization override rule for NONCLINICAL+impurity co-occurrence.
implementation_notes.threshold_recommendation: For production classification: require >= 1 HIGH confidence signal OR >= 2 MEDIUM confidence signals for category assignment. DEFINITIVE signals alone are sufficient.
implementation_notes.multi_label_note: CRLs routinely contain multiple deficiency categories. All rules should be applied independently — a single CRL may trigger CAT-11-A + CAT-07 + CAT-04-H simultaneously.
implementation_notes.section_header_extraction: Pre-processing step: extract all uppercase section headers from CRL text as the primary structural signal. CRL section headers are consistent FDA templates and are the most reliable classification signal available.
